title: David Sinclair Calls for Saving HAGR Aging Research Databases
description: David Sinclair highlighted the critical importance of preserving the HAGR databases (GenAge, AnAge, GenDR) which underpin computational aging research but face a funding gap.

title: New Research: Protecting Telomeres Could Slow Biological Aging
description: New research suggests that protecting telomeres, the DNA caps linked to cellular aging, could slow biological aging and represents another step toward understanding longevity mechanisms.

title: New Documentary Examines Longevity Research and the Shifting Public Conversation on Aging
description: A new documentary featuring leading geroscientists explores lifestyle, longevity research, and how the public conversation around aging is fundamentally shifting.

title: ReVision Implant Gets FDA Breakthrough Device Designation for Vision Restoration
description: ReVision Implant received FDA Breakthrough Device designation for a neural interface designed to restore vision by directly stimulating the visual cortex.

title: New AI Platform Translates Complex Health Data Into Personalized Women's Health Guidance
description: A clinically grounded AI system aims to translate complex wearable and health data into personalized guidance for women's health, integrating with Oura and other platforms.

title: Global Data: People Rank Sleep Above Diet and Exercise, Yet Fail to Act
description: New global health data reveals people rank sleep as their top health priority above diet and exercise, yet most still fail to address chronic sleep problems.

title: Insilico Medicine and Liquid AI Launch Secure AI for Drug Research
description: Insilico Medicine partners with Liquid AI to launch a secure, lightweight AI platform to streamline drug research and development for longevity therapeutics.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Overview of top longevity biotechs including Altos Labs ($3B), Cambrian Bio ($1.79B valuation), Genflow Biosciences (gene therapy), and Junevity (siRNA cell reset therapeutics).

title: GMP Production Begins for Alpha-Klotho Longevity Protein Therapy
description: Production begins under GMP standards for a scalable anti-aging therapy built around the alpha-Klotho longevity protein, advancing Klothonova's cell-based therapeutic approach.

title: Orthopedic Surgeon: Aging Bodies Must Be Prepared for Load, Not Protected From It
description: Orthopedic surgeon Howard Luks argues that strength training removes risk from the future, and the risks of sedentary behavior far outweigh those of resistance training in older adults.

title: Vesalic Targets Neurodegeneration Upstream After ALS Blood Discovery
description: Vesalic CEO discusses targeting neurodegenerative disease upstream after discovering a metabolic dysfunction in the blood of ALS patients, opening new therapeutic avenues.

title: Dr. Rhonda Patrick: What You Eat Today Shapes Your Sleep Tonight
description: New research shows higher-fiber, plant-diverse days improve deep and REM sleep, while processed foods increase nighttime wakefulness. A 4-hour gap between dinner and sleep lowers overnight heart rate.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytics, Stem Cells, and Brain Aging
description: Monthly roundup covers senolytic effectiveness via mitochondrial stress (Nature Aging), stem cell therapy for aging frailty (Phase 2b), and resistance exercise slowing brain aging clocks.

title: Next-Gen Senotherapeutics: AI/ML-Driven Strategies for Aging Drug Discovery
description: New review covers AI-driven discovery of novel senotherapeutics using advanced computational techniques to identify and optimize drugs targeting cellular senescence.

title: Rapamycin: Benefits of Life Extension Vary Widely Between Individuals
description: A review of 167 studies found rapamycin and caloric restriction extend lifespan on average, but individual responses vary dramatically -- some gain years, others see no benefit at all.

title: Norn Group: Industrializing Longevity Research With $34M in Grants
description: Norn Group has allocated $34M through Impetus Grants to target bottlenecks in longevity research, plus talent programs and a clinical trial framework for aging interventions.

title: Longevity Investing 2026: $30.7B Market Projected to Reach $52.8B by 2035
description: Strategic guide for family offices outlines the longevity biotech market at $30.7B in 2026, driven by maturing epigenetic reprogramming, advancing senolytics, and AI-driven drug discovery.

title: Top 10 Longevity Breakthroughs of 2025: PEARL Trial, GLP-1s, SGLT2 Inhibitors
description: Review of 2025's biggest longevity advances including the PEARL rapamycin trial showing lean mass benefits in women, GLP-1s as potential longevity drugs, and SGLT2 inhibitors lengthening telomeres.

title: VITAL-H: First Phase 3 Human Clinical Trial Targeting Aging Awarded $38M
description: The Barshop Institute's VITAL-H trial will test rapamycin, dapagliflozin (SGLT2i), and semaglutide (GLP-1) to delay age-related decline in adults aged 60-65, marking the first Phase 3 trial targeting aging itself.

title: Cambrian Biopharma Secures $30.8M PROSPR Award for Next-Gen mTOR Inhibitor TOR-101
description: Cambrian Biopharma's TOR-101, a highly selective mTORC1 inhibitor with strong safety data in primates, will advance to human clinical trials with $30.8M in ARPA-H PROSPR funding.

title: TRIIM-X Trial Shows 2.82-Year Epigenetic Age Reversal With Thymus Protocol
description: Results from the TRIIM-X trial show a combo of growth hormone, DHEA, metformin, zinc, and vitamin D3 produced a 2.82-year reduction in epigenetic age via Horvath clock in 65 participants with no adverse effects.

title: ARPA-H Awards Seven Research Teams to Extend Healthy Years of Life
description: Federal grant funds TPN-101 studies aimed at helping people stay healthier as they age, part of the ARPA-H PROSPR program targeting healthspan extension through novel biomarkers and therapies.

title: Study: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by 0.1-0.2 Years
description: Older adults taking a daily multivitamin for 2 years showed reduced pace of epigenetic aging vs placebo, with the strongest effect in those with accelerated biological aging. Cocoa flavanols showed no effect.

title: Longevity Market: 81 Funding Deals Mapped -- Retro Bio's $1B Leads
description: Comprehensive mapping of 81 longevity funding deals shows Retro Biosciences' $1B as the largest since Altos Labs. Epigenetic reprogramming is the most funded approach, with NewLimit raising $175M backed by Eli Lilly.

title: Life Biosciences Begins First Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: David Sinclair's Life Biosciences received FDA approval for the first-ever human trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming, targeting vision restoration in glaucoma and NAION patients.
